CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100));
START TRANSACTION READ WRITE;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Alice');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (2, 'Bob');
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'A%');
UPDATE test_table SET name = 'Charlie' WHERE id = 1;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_table;
CREATE TABLE temp_table AS SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id > 1;
COMMIT;
START TRANSACTION READ ONLY;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE id < (SELECT AVG(id) FROM test_table);
ROLLBACK;
START TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL SERIALIZABLE;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) SELECT id + 100, CONCAT(name, '_updated') FROM test_table WHERE id = 2;
SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_table);
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD COLUMN age INT;
UPDATE test_table SET age = 30 WHERE name = 'Alice';
SELECT DISTINCT name FROM test_table WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM temp_table WHERE test_table.id = temp_table.id);
DROP TABLE test_table;
COMMIT;
